disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden onset of profound whole-body heaviness, strained/whispered voice and slowed foggy thinking immediately on returning home from a prolonged work event
trigger_question_examples: What exactly are you feeling right now?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It came on all at once the moment I got home tonight. My body feels like lead, my voice is just a whisper, and my brain feels like it shut off.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No facial drooping, no one-sided arm or leg weakness, no slurred speech or difficulty swallowing, no loss of vision (patient can screen these on direct questioning)
trigger_question_examples: Is one side of your face drooping, or is one arm or leg weak?
Are you slurring your words or having trouble swallowing, or has your vision changed?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — my face feels normal, both sides of my body feel equally heavy rather than one side, I can swallow, and my vision is fine. It's my voice being gone and this total exhaustion.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Symptom pattern is tightly tied to prolonged effortful self-monitoring of speech and behaviour at social/professional events and eases with extended solitude
trigger_question_examples: Has this happened before, and is there a pattern to when it hits?
What was the evening like before this came on?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It happens after these big events where I spend hours carefully changing how I speak and act to fit in. Tonight was a long one. When I finally get home and can be alone and silent, I crash like this.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Tense, sore anterior neck and throat after sustained altered voice use; no difficulty breathing and no drooling
trigger_question_examples: Does your neck or throat feel tense or sore?
Are you having any trouble breathing or any drooling?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My neck and throat are tight and achy from holding my voice a certain way all night, but I can breathe fine and I'm not drooling.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Persistent sense of a fragmented authentic self, need for prolonged isolation after interactions, and resentment at having to modify behaviour to feel safe; no current thoughts of self-harm or suicide on sensitive enquiry
trigger_question_examples: How has all of this been affecting your mood and how you feel about yourself?
Have you had any thoughts that life isn't worth living or of hurting yourself?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Honestly I feel worn down and like I've lost who I really am, and I need to hide away from everyone afterwards. I haven't had any thoughts of harming myself, but I feel really burnt out and alone.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I don't really want to get into the heavy stuff — can we just focus on why my body crashed like this?
